title: Round to the Nearest Perfect Square

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.outputFormat: The nearest perfect square to n.
statement.notes: This is where most interviewers will look for your thought process and basic understanding of perfect squares.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: To find the nearest perfect square, we can calculate the square root of the number and round it to the nearest integer.
editorial.optimalSolution: We can calculate the square root of the number and round it to the nearest integer using a for loop and the sqrt function.

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int solve(int n); };
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public int solve(int n); }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, n: int) -> int:

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int solve(int n) { return n * n; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public int solve(int n) { return n * n; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, n: int) -> int: return n * n

solutions.python:
import math
import sys

class Solution:
    def solve(self, n: int) -> int:
        # Calculate the square root of n
        sqrt_n = math.sqrt(n)

        # Round the square root to the nearest integer
        nearest_square = round(sqrt_n)

        # Return the nearest perfect square
        return nearest_square


if __name__ == '__main__':

    # Read the number of test cases
    t = int(input())

    # Iterate over each test case
    for _ in range(t):

        # Read the number
        n = int(input())

        # Create an instance of the Solution class
        sol = Solution()

        # Print the result of solve(n)
        print(sol.solve(n))
